_comment: Canonical Voxhelm changelog. Single source of truth: the release script (release.ps1) turns the newest entry's notes into latest.json for the in-app 'Update & Restart' prompt, and the website /changelog page renders this same file (publish-update.sh copies it to Jarvis_website/src/data/releases.json). THIS IS PUBLISHED MARKETING COPY, NOT AN ENGINEERING LOG — competitors and prospects read every line. Never describe a security fix's mechanism, never admit a defect in the payment or sign-in path, never name vendors/architecture (Stripe, CDP, providers, the broker), never hint at what's coming next. Say what the customer can now DO; collapse everything else into 'Reliability improvements'. TERSE IS THE HOUSE STYLE — size the note to the size of the change: a small release gets ONE short line ('Voxhelm has a new app icon.'), never a paragraph. Do not explain the change, tour the user through where they will see it, or reassure them the rest still works. Only ship an entry for a release that changed something worth a customer's attention — patch releases need no entry. The candid record lives in docs/_milestone_log.md, which is not published. See CLAUDE.md, 'The changelog is a public surface'. Add new entries at the TOP. Dates are ISO YYYY-MM-DD.

releases.title: See Voxhelm working
notes: A new status capsule shows what Voxhelm is doing at a glance — listening, heard you, thinking, acting, speaking.
Longer tasks now show their progress live: the goal, each step as it happens, and how to cancel.
On-screen drawing looks sharper: colors that carry meaning, labels that stay readable on any background, smooth erasing, and a new spotlight to focus your attention.

releases.title: Meeting notes fixes
notes: Taking notes from the Meetings page now works reliably.

releases.title: Meeting notes
notes: New: Voxhelm writes up your meetings for you. Turn it on in Settings › Meetings, then say “take notes for this meeting” or press Take notes on the Meetings page. Works with Teams, Meet, Zoom and the rest, and nothing joins your call.

The moment the meeting ends you get the document: what was decided, who agreed to what, the questions left open, and the full transcript. It's saved as a Markdown file in your own folder. Export to other formats is coming.
It finishes on its own once the meeting does, so you don't have to remember.

releases.title: Dictate anywhere
notes: New: hold Right Ctrl and speak — Voxhelm types what you said, polished, wherever your cursor is. Works in every app, in your language.
Pick an output style and add names it should spell your way in Settings → Dictation.

releases.title: New icon
notes: Voxhelm has a new app icon.

releases.title: A clear acceptable-use policy
notes: Voxhelm now politely declines requests that go against our acceptable-use policy - unlawful or harmful actions. Everything else works exactly as it always has.
The Terms of Service at voxhelm.de/terms now spell out the policy in plain language.

releases.title: Voxhelm remembers you
notes: Tell Voxhelm something about yourself - "remember that I'm practising German at B1", "I edit video in Premiere", "keep your replies short" - and it still knows next time you talk to it. No more starting from scratch every session.
Say "forget that I..." whenever something changes, or just tell it the new version - it updates what it knows instead of piling up contradictions.
New Memory panel in the app: see everything Voxhelm has learned about you, edit or delete any of it, or switch memory off. It's kept on your PC only, and Voxhelm won't remember passwords or keys.

releases.title: No more hourly cut-off
notes: The hourly limit is gone. Your voice time is now measured daily and weekly only - so a long session no longer gets cut short just because you've been busy in the last hour.
Your usage now shows what actually matters: your daily limit and your weekly limit.

releases.title: See your Pro trial at a glance
notes: Your free Pro trial now shows the days remaining, right in the sidebar - so you always know where you stand.
Keeping Pro is one click from there when you're ready, and nothing you've got used to goes away.
Already on Pro? Elite, with three times the voice limits, is now a click away too.

releases.title: Plans that make sense: Free, Pro, and the new Elite
notes: Three clear plans: Free, Pro ($15/month), and the new Elite ($30/month) with three times the Pro voice limits. Prices show in euros or dollars depending on where you are.

Manage your subscription right in the app: move up to Elite, switch back down, or cancel to Free. Cancelling keeps everything you paid for until the period ends, and you can resume any time before then.
The plans page shows your current plan and when it renews - no more guessing what you're on.

releases.title: Answers from the live web, and your browser stays yours
notes: Ask Voxhelm to check, look up, or find anything online and it answers straight away from a live web search.
Ask about the latest anything - models, prices, releases, news - and you get today's answer, not last year's.
'Open the browser' opens YOUR Chrome, signed in as you, and asking for a site opens it in your default browser.

releases.title: A lighter touch
notes: The line you draw when circling something on screen now fades away behind your cursor like smoke, instead of staying as a solid mark.
Same gesture, same answers - only the look changed.

releases.title: Show Voxhelm what you're looking at
notes: Point at your screen. Hold Right Alt to talk, then hold your right mouse button and circle anything - a paragraph, a diagram, an icon, a spot on a page. Voxhelm sees exactly what you circled and answers about it, or acts right there.
Circle a muscle in a diagram and ask "what is this?". Circle a paragraph and ask what it says. Circle a spot on your web page and say "add a button right here".
Your right-click is untouched unless you're holding the talk key - and you can switch the whole thing off in Settings.
New Guide section in the app: the shortcuts that matter, and a bank of things to say out loud. Most people never discover half of what Voxhelm can do.

releases.title: You're always the one who decides
notes: Voxhelm now checks with you before it changes anything on your machine - and the confirmation shows you exactly what it's about to do, and where.
Security and safety improvements throughout.

releases.title: Hands-on setup, and a free Pro trial
notes: Setup now walks you through it hands-on: hold Right Alt to talk, ask Voxhelm to look something up and type it for you, and have it draw on your screen - so you know it works before you start.
Your Pro trial is on us. Just sign in, no card needed. After that you stay on a free plan with generous limits.
'Check for updates' shows you the result right in Settings, instead of a message you might miss.

releases.title: Automatic updates
notes: Voxhelm now updates itself - when a new version ships you get a quiet 'Update & Restart' prompt instead of re-downloading the installer.
